message:
Fix: skip auto-flush when --no-db mode is active

When --no-db flag is set, the storage is an in-memory SQLite instance
backed by JSONL import/export. Auto-flush would fail because there's no
persistent database to flush from. This fix adds a check for cli.no_db
to the auto-flush condition, preventing spurious errors when operating
in JSONL-only mode.

The auto-flush mechanism is designed to export dirty issues to JSONL
after mutating commands, but this is unnecessary and problematic when
the database is ephemeral. The JSONL handling is already managed by
the StorageContext::flush_no_db_if_dirty() method in no-db mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
